copyright_note: Internal reference only. Never served to end users.

items.text: Every Saturday, our local farmer's market is the place to be! Fresh fruits, veggies, homemade goodies, and unique crafts await you. The Thompson family's organic produce is a must-try, known for its quality and cordial service. Don't miss the bakery stall — get there early for the best bread and pastries, including gluten-free and vegan options. The smell of fresh bread fills the air, and treats disappear quickly! In addition to food, the market sells handmade crafts like jewelry, pottery, and textiles. These unique items make perfect gifts and support local artisans. Plus, enjoy live music while you shop.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the post?

options.A: To explain the benefits of organic farming
options.B: To describe the variety of products available at the farmer's market
options.C: To compare different farmer's markets in the area
options.D: To offer advice on starting a stall

questions.explanation: The writer describes many stalls — produce, bakery items, crafts, and music — to show the market's variety.

questions.stem: What reason is given for the popularity of the Thompson family's stall?

options.A: They offer cooking tips and recipes
options.C: They provide friendly service and excellent products
options.D: They have a beautiful, well-decorated stall

questions.explanation: The post describes the Thompson family's produce as 'known for its quality and cordial service.'

questions.stem: Why do customers go to the bakery stall early?

options.A: To get free samples given in mornings
options.B: To get freshly baked bread and pastries before they are gone
options.D: To take advantage of early morning discounts

questions.explanation: The post says to 'get there early' because 'treats disappear quickly.'

items.text:
Dear Student Council,

Our school has been invited to participate in the annual City Clean-Up Day on May 5th. We need 50 student volunteers to help clean Riverside Park from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM.

Volunteers will receive:
• Community service hours
• Free lunch
• A commemorative t-shirt

There will be a sign-up meeting this Thursday at lunch in Room 201. If you can't attend but want to help, email Mr. Davis by Friday.

This is a great chance to represent our school and help the environment!

Thanks,
Martin Lee

questions.explanation: The email's primary purpose is to recruit 50 student volunteers for the City Clean-Up Day.
The email lists community service hours, free lunch, and a commemorative t-shirt as benefits.
The email states the City Clean-Up Day is 'on May 5th.'

items.text:
Dear Ms. Edwards,

The reservation for the art workshop that you made on September 10th has been confirmed. The workshop will take place on September 20th at 3:00 PM. All necessary art supplies will be provided, but please bring your own apron or smock.

Best regards,
Laura Bennett

questions.stem: When is the date of the art workshop?
questions.explanation: The email states 'The workshop will take place on September 20th.' September 10th was the reservation date.

questions.stem: What should Ms. Edwards bring to the workshop?
questions.explanation: The email asks to 'bring your own apron or smock,' a protective garment.

items.text:
From: Omar Haddad
To: Lila Nguyen

The maintenance department just called to confirm that our balcony will be repainted tomorrow at 9:00 a.m. I'll be at an early concert rehearsal, so could you be home to unlock the balcony door and let the crew inside? They said the floor must be clear. You should bring the plants and drying rack inside tonight. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why did Omar write to Lila?

options.A: To invite her to a concert rehearsal
options.B: To tell her about a maintenance visit
options.C: To complain about a noisy crew

questions.explanation: Omar writes to inform Lila about the balcony repainting and ask for her help with preparations.

questions.stem: What does Omar ask Lila to do tonight?

options.C: Call to confirm the arrival time
options.D: Cover the balcony floor with plastic

questions.explanation: Omar asks Lila to 'bring the plants and drying rack inside tonight.' Unlocking the door is for tomorrow morning.

items.text: The University Bookstore will hold its annual textbook buyback event from May 15 to May 20. Due to ongoing electrical repairs at the main bookstore, the buyback station will be located in the Student Center Atrium, directly across from the Campus ID Office. Books with moderate highlighting and notes are accepted; however, the bookstore cannot purchase books with water damage, broken bindings, or missing covers. A valid student ID card is required to complete any transaction. Operating hours are 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this announcement?

options.A: To announce a temporary closure of the main bookstore
options.B: To inform students about selling used textbooks for cash
options.C: To provide instructions for repairing damaged textbooks
options.D: To advertise a sale on new textbooks

questions.explanation: The notice announces a textbook buyback event where students can sell used textbooks.

questions.stem: Where will the buyback event take place?
questions.explanation: The notice states the buyback station will be in the Student Center Atrium.

questions.stem: Which type of book will the bookstore NOT accept?
questions.explanation: The notice states the bookstore 'cannot purchase books with water damage, broken bindings, or missing covers.'

items.text:
Jamal (3:28 P.M.): Hey, have you prepared the handouts for our presentation this evening?

Sofia (3:30 P.M.): Not yet. There was a long line at the library printer when I stopped by earlier.

Jamal (3:31 P.M.): OK, no worries. We just need them before 4:15 so we can review everything one last time before heading to the classroom.

Sofia (3:32 P.M.): I'll head back in about 10 minutes to try again. I'll text you as soon as I've got the copies. If the printer's still occupied, I'll check the one in the student center.

Jamal (3:33 P.M.): Great! Thanks for handling it! Let's meet near Room 210 around 4:00.

Sofia (3:34 P.M.): Perfect. See you there!

questions.stem: What is the main reason Sofia plans to return to the library?

options.A: To meet Jamal near the classroom
options.B: To check if she can use the printer

questions.explanation: Sofia says she will 'head back' to 'try again' because the printer was occupied earlier.

questions.stem: Why might Sofia visit the student center?

options.B: To print handouts for the presentation

questions.explanation: Sofia says 'If the printer's still occupied, I'll check the one in the student center.'

questions.stem: What does Jamal suggest they do before the presentation?

options.A: Add more content to their copies
options.C: Have a final review of the materials
options.D: Reserve an appointment at the library printer

questions.explanation: Jamal says they need the handouts so they 'can review everything one last time.'

items.text:
Dear Ms. Nguyen,

We're excited to invite you and your friends and family to the grand opening of our new branch at 25 Orchid Street, happening next Monday. This event is a great opportunity to explore our state-of-the-art facility, featuring top-tier equipment, energizing group classes, and expert personal training.

This celebration, exclusively for our valued members and their guests, will include guided tours, live fitness demonstrations, and complimentary refreshments.

Bring your friends and family! We're offering a special one-day-only discount on memberships for all attendees.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the email?

options.A: To attract customers to a new fitness center
options.B: To provide Ms. Nguyen with expert training
options.C: To celebrate Ms. Nguyen's wellness achievements
options.D: To announce a discount for existing members

questions.explanation: The email invites Ms. Nguyen to a grand opening with tours, demos, and a membership discount.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Ms. Nguyen's relationship with the fitness center?

options.B: She has already explored the new facility
options.C: She is a member at one of the other locations
options.D: She is new to using fitness centers

questions.explanation: The email calls her a 'valued member' and invites her to a 'new branch,' implying she is already a member elsewhere.
